question: Is the image a horizontal layout with a white background containing two diagrams arranged side by side (P-V on the left, T-S on the right)?
Are the two diagrams separated by a thin gray vertical line?
Do both diagrams feature black coordinate axes with consistent tick marks?
Does the left diagram (P-V) contain a closed cycle formed by two smooth blue isothermal curves (hyperbola-like) and two steeper blue adiabatic curves?
In the left diagram, is the upper isothermal curve labeled 'TH' and the lower one labeled 'TC'?
In the left diagram, are there four blue solid dots labeled 1 (bottom-left), 2 (bottom-right), 3 (top-right), and 4 (top-left)?
In the left diagram, does the cycle follow the specific path: 1→2 (lower isotherm), 2→3 (right adiabatic), 3→4 (upper isotherm), and 4→1 (left adiabatic)?
In the left diagram, do the arrows indicate a counterclockwise direction (1→2→3→4)?
Does the right diagram (T-S) depict a blue rectangular cycle with vertical left and right sides?
In the right diagram, is the top horizontal line labeled 'TH' and the bottom horizontal line labeled 'TC'?
In the right diagram, are the corner points labeled 1, 2, 3, 4 with the same positioning as the left diagram (1 bottom-left, 2 bottom-right, 3 top-right, 4 top-left)?
Do the arrows in the right diagram indicate a clockwise direction?
Is there a thin gray bracket labeled 'QH' above the top of the right diagram and a label 'QC' below the bottom?
Is the area inside the right rectangular cycle annotated with 'W' in thin gray text?
Is the overall color scheme strictly limited to black, gray, and blue?
Is the image style flat with uniform line width, free of shadows or gradients?
Is all text content legible and limited to Latin letters and numerals (no garbled text or non-Latin characters)?
